column10: Quote

column7: John Tuld (Jeremy Irons) and Sam Rogers
column10: "There are three ways to make a living in this business: be first, be smarter, or cheat." — John Tuld
"I may have been early, but I'm not wrong." — Michael Burry
"Follow the money." — Deep Throat
"There is a storm coming like nothing you have ever seen! And not a one of you is prepared for it!" — Curtis

column4: The Lives of Others / Das Leben der Anderen
column10: "Schreibmaschine — keine." ("Typewriter — none.") — Wiesler's falsified report

"Toute ressemblance avec des événements réels, des personnes mortes ou vivantes n'est pas le fait du hasard. Elle est VOLONTAIRE." ("Any resemblance to real events or persons living or dead is not coincidental. It is INTENTIONAL.") — opening title card
"Adventure is out there!" — Ellie / Carl
"I never had any friends later on like the ones I had when I was twelve. Jesus, does anyone?" — The Writer (Gordie)
"What we found out is that each one of us is a brain… and an athlete… and a basket case… a princess… and a criminal." — Brian Johnson (closing letter)

column7: CC Bloom (Bette Midler) and Hillary Whitney
column10: "Did I ever tell you you're my hero? You're everything I would like to be." — "Wind Beneath My Wings," sung by CC
"Laughter through tears is my favorite emotion." — Truvy Jones

column7: Tre Styles (Cuba Gooding Jr.), Doughboy (Ice Cube), Ricky
column10: "Either they don't know, don't show, or don't care about what's going on in the hood." — Doughboy
"I'm older and I have more insurance!" — Evelyn Couch

column7: Sarah, Harold, Sam, Michael, Karen, Nick, Meg, Chloe
column8: Glenn Close, Kevin Kline, Tom Berenger, Jeff Goldblum, JoBeth Williams, William Hurt, Mary Kay Place, Meg Tilly
column10: "I haven't met that many happy people in my life. How do they act?" — Nick

column7: Aibileen Clark (Viola Davis), Minny Jackson (Octavia Spencer), Skeeter
column10: "You is kind. You is smart. You is important." — Aibileen Clark
"There's no crying in baseball!" — Jimmy Dugan

column7: William Miller (Patrick Fugit), Penny Lane (Kate Hudson), Russell Hammond
column10: "I am a golden god!" — Russell Hammond / "If you ever get lonely, just go to the record store and visit your friends." — Lester Bangs (paraphrase of confidant role)

column4: Amélie / Le Fabuleux Destin d'Amélie Poulain
column7: Amélie Poulain (Audrey Tautou); Raymond Dufayel ("Verre Cassé")
column10: "Cette fille a le cœur fragile, c'est la seule de la bande qui voit l'intérieur des gens." — Raymond Dufayel (about the girl with the glass — and about Amélie)
"First, observe the whole bowl. Appreciate its gestalt." — Old Master to apprentice (paraphrase from the film's ramen-etiquette interlude)

column4: Like Water for Chocolate / Como agua para chocolate
column10: "A la mesa y a la cama, una sola vez se llama." ("To the table and to bed, one is called only once.") — Mama Elena (against which Tita's affiliative kitchen circle defines itself)
"Always resignation and acceptance. Always prudence and honour and duty. Elinor, where is your heart?" — Marianne to Elinor

column7: Joo-ri / 주리 (Kim Hye-jun / 김혜준) and Yoon-a / 윤아
column10: "우리 둘이 어떻게든 해보자." / "Let's the two of us figure something out, somehow." — Joo-ri to Yoon-a (paraphrase / 의역 — final hospital sequence)
"This car. Goeth would have bought this car. Why did I keep the car? Ten people right there. Ten people. Ten more people." — Oskar Schindler

column4: Of Gods and Men / Des hommes et des dieux
column10: "If it should happen one day — and it could be today — that I become a victim of the terrorism that now seems to encompass all the foreigners living in Algeria, I would like my community, my Church, my family, to remember that my life was given to God and to this country." — Frère Christian's testament
"I want the last thing you see in this world to be a face of love." — Sister Helen
"I'm not going to give up on him." — Augusto Odone
"You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view... until you climb into his skin and walk around in it." — Atticus Finch
"If might is right, then love has no place in the world. It may be so, it may be so. But I don't have the strength to live in a world like that." — Father Gabriel
"I miss my father, I miss everything." — Dora's letter to Josué
"I want to send people on their way with care." — Sasaki, the senior encoffiner (paraphrase from the film)

column7: Dr. Wilbur Larch (Michael Caine); Homer Wells
column10: "Good night, you princes of Maine, you kings of New England!" — Dr. Larch
"It has to be something really big, something they can't do by themselves. So I do it for them, then they do it for three other people." — Trevor

column4: Three Colours: Blue / Trois couleurs: Bleu
column10: "Now I have only one thing left to do: nothing. I don't want any belongings, any memories. No friends, no love. Those are all traps." — Julie (early in the film, against which her later turn to altruism defines itself)

column7: Susie Monahan, RN (Audra McDonald); Vivian Bearing
column10: "She's no-code. She's no-code!" — Susie Monahan, fighting off the resuscitation team
"우리가 싸우는 건 세상을 바꾸기 위해서가 아니라, 세상이 우리를 바꾸지 못하게 하려는 겁니다." / "We fight not to change the world, but so the world doesn't change us." — Seo Yoo-jin (activist co-protagonist) to In-ho

column4: Life Is Beautiful / La vita è bella
column10: "You can lose all your points for any one of three things. One: If you cry. Two: If you ask to see your mother. Three: If you're hungry and ask for a snack! Forget it!" — Guido (translating the guard's rules)

column4: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
column7: President Merkin Muffley (Peter Sellers); Group Captain Mandrake (Sellers); Dr. Strangelove
column10: "Gentlemen, you can't fight in here! This is the War Room!" — President Muffley

column7: Capt. Hawkeye Pierce (Donald Sutherland); Capt. Trapper John
column10: "Suicide is painless. It brings on many changes." — film's theme, sung at Painless's "Last Supper"
"We think too much and feel too little. More than machinery we need humanity… Look up, Hannah!" — The Jewish Barber

column4: To Be or Not to Be
column10: "So they call me 'Concentration Camp' Ehrhardt!" — Col. Ehrhardt (Sig Ruman) / repeated as a joke by Tura impersonating him

"There's an old joke. Two elderly women are at a Catskill mountain resort, and one of 'em says, 'Boy, the food at this place is really terrible.' The other one says, 'Yeah, I know — and such small portions.' Well, that's essentially how I feel about life." — Alvy Singer
"Dance is for people who are free. It's an escape from all this." — Rosie Betzler
"You treat a disease, you win, you lose. You treat a person, I guarantee you, you'll win, no matter what the outcome." — Patch Adams
"A lot of people enjoy being dead. But they are not dead, really. They're just backing away from life. Reach out. Take a chance. Get hurt even." — Maude
"I'm the preeminent Proust scholar in the United States." — Frank Ginsberg

column4: Train of Life / Train de vie
column10: "We will deport ourselves." — Mordechai (paraphrase of the village council's plan)

column7: Lavrentiy Beria (Simon Russell Beale); Khrushchev (Steve Buscemi); Molotov
column10: "How can you run, plot, and shoot at the same time?" — Khrushchev
"He was my North, my South, my East and West. My working week and my Sunday rest." — Matthew (W.H. Auden's "Funeral Blues")
"Words with a 'k' in it are funny. Alka-Seltzer is funny. Chicken is funny. Pickle is funny. All with a 'k'. 'L's are not funny. 'M's are not funny." — Willy Clark
"이것은 갈비인가, 통닭인가." / "Is this galbi, or is it fried chicken?" — Detective Ma (마형사)
"밥은 먹고 다니냐?" / "Do you eat meals — out there?" — Park Doo-man, to the suspect (Park Hae-il)

column4: Sympathy for Lady Vengeance / 친절한 금자씨
column10: "너나 잘하세요." / "Mind your own business." (lit. "Look after yourself.") — Geum-ja
"I'm staying right where I am. It's gonna take you, and the police department, and the fire department, and the National Guard to get me out of here!" — Norma Rae
"It's not so easy for me to raise my hand and send a boy off to die without talking about it first." — Juror #8
"They're called boobs, Ed." — Erin Brockovich
"All you have to do is tell the truth." — Bill White (to Josey, articulating the defense she chooses)
"My name is Paikea Apirana, and I come from a long line of chiefs stretching all the way back to the Whale Rider." — Pai
"I want the truth!" — Kaffee
"All you need is ganas. Desire." — Jaime Escalante
"I think that we — and I am being very specific in answering you — manipulate, we control nicotine delivery." — Jeffrey Wigand
"Eat my shit." — Minny Jackson (direct line in confrontation with Hilly, paraphrase of "Eat my shit" cake-reveal scene)
"Feelings are scary. Sometimes they're painful. And if you can't feel pain, you're not gonna feel anything else either." — Dr. Berger
"It's not your fault." — Sean Maguire (catalysing Will's self-observation)
"A doctor's first duty is to ask forgiveness." — examiner in Isak's dream (the indictment he is finally able to recognise as his own diagnosis)
"I just don't know what I'm supposed to be." — Charlotte
"Don't you think maybe they are the same thing? Love and attention?" — Sister Sarah Joan
"What if I forgive myself? What if I was sorry, but if I could go back in time I wouldn't do a single thing differently?" — Cheryl Strayed

column4: The Diving Bell and the Butterfly / Le scaphandre et le papillon
column10: "I decided to stop pitying myself. Other than my eye, two things aren't paralyzed: my imagination and my memory." — Jean-Dominique Bauby
"I'm fat. I'm bald. I'm repulsive… I should just go upstairs and write." — Charlie Kaufman
"I will be dying and so will you, and so will everyone here." — Caden Cotard
"Accept me as I am — only thus can I discover myself." — Guido (closing voiceover, paraphrase of the line as commonly translated)
"She never grows up… Marcee can't be real. She never grows up." — John Nash (the moment of recognition that anchors his self-observation strategy)

column4: Eat Drink Man Woman / Yin shi nan nu
column10: "We communicate by eating." — Jia-Chien (paraphrase of theme line)

column4: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)
column10: "You confuse love for admiration." — Sam Thomson (his daughter, articulating what Riggan can finally see in himself)
"I can't beat it. I can't beat it. I'm sorry." — Lee Chandler
"나 다시 돌아갈래!" / "I want to go back!" — Kim Yong-ho, on the railway bridge

column7: Eun-hee / 은희 (Park Ji-hu / 박지후); 한문 teacher Kim Young-ji / 김영지
column10: "은희야, 힘들고 우울할 땐, 손가락을 봐. 그리고 한 손가락, 한 손가락, 움직여. 그럼 참 신비롭게 느껴진다." / "Eun-hee, when you feel hopeless and depressed — look at your fingers. Move them, one by one. Then you'll feel something mysterious." — Young-ji 선생님, in her final letter

column7: Yoo Hae-guk / 유해국 (Park Hae-il / 박해일); Cheon Yong-deok 이장
column10: "니는 신이 될라 캤나? 내는 인간이 될라 캤다." / "Did you want to become a god? I just wanted to become human." — Cheon Yong-deok 이장 to Yoo Mok-hyung (and by extension to Hae-guk)
"I don't want a childhood. I want to be a ballet dancer." — Billy Elliot
"I'd rather die drunk, broke at 34 and have people at a dinner table talk about me than live to be rich and sober at 90." — Andrew Neiman
"You never got me down, Ray. You never got me down." — Jake LaMotta
"I just want to be perfect." — Nina Sayers

column8: Geoffrey Rush; Noah Taylor as young David
column10: "It's a tragic life, David. Tragic. It's only music that will keep us together." — Peter Helfgott (the father whose threat David sublimates into the music itself)
"What are you?" — Captain Hosenfeld. "I am... I was... a pianist." — Szpilman

column7: Antonio Salieri (F. Murray Abraham); Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
column10: "Forgive me, Majesty. I am a vulgar man! But I assure you, my music is not." — Mozart

column7: Salvatore "Totò" Di Vita (Salvatore Cascio young, Marco Leonardi teen, Jacques Perrin adult); Alfredo
column10: "Whatever you end up doing, love it. The way you loved the projection booth when you were a little squirt." — Alfredo
"Why do you want to dance?" — Lermontov. "Why do you want to live?" — Vicky Page
"I paint myself because I am so often alone and because I am the subject I know best." — Frida Kahlo (text from the film, drawn from Kahlo's own writings)
"We are your symphony, Mr. Holland." — Gertrude Lang (the former student turned governor)
"Anyone can cook!" — Auguste Gusteau (Remy's idol, articulating the principle Remy embodies)
"Dance, dance, otherwise we are lost." — Pina Bausch
"I'm not a prophet, but I know that our people will keep going forward, all together, with all of our strength." — Pai

column4: Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter… and Spring / 봄 여름 가을 겨울 그리고 봄
column7: The young monk in adulthood / 청년 스님 (Kim Young-min, then Kim Ki-duk himself in winter); the old master / 노스님
column10: "분노는 분노로 사라지지 않는다. 너는 그 분노를 새겨야 한다." / "Anger is not extinguished by anger. You must carve that anger out." — the old master, instructing the disciple to chisel the Heart Sutra into the temple deck (paraphrase / 의역; close to the spoken instruction)

"그 곳은 어떤가요. 얼마나 적막한가요. … 검은 강물을 건너기 전에 내 영혼의 마지막 숨을 다해 나는 기도합니다." / "How is it where you are? How quiet is it there? … Before crossing the black river, with the last breath of my soul, I pray." — from "아네스의 노래," read by Mi-ja in voice-over (and then by the dead girl's voice)

column7: Choi Gon / 최곤 (Park Joong-hoon / 박중훈); manager Park Min-su / 박민수
column10: "별은 말이지, 혼자 빛나는 별은 없어. 다 빛을 받아서 빛나는 거야." / "Stars — there's no such thing as a star that shines alone. Every star shines with light reflected from another." — Park Min-su, to Choi Gon

"Ilsa, I'm no good at being noble, but it doesn't take much to see that the problems of three little people don't amount to a hill of beans in this crazy world. Someday you'll understand that. … Here's looking at you, kid." — Rick Blaine

"This can't last. This misery can't last. I must remember that and try to control myself. Nothing lasts really. Neither happiness nor despair. Not even life lasts very long." — Laura Jesson (interior monologue)
"A great butler must be possessed of dignity… in keeping with his position." — Stevens (to his father)

column4: In the Mood for Love / 花樣年華
column7: Chow Mo-wan (Tony Leung) and Su Li-zhen / Mrs. Chan
column10: "I thought we wouldn't be like them. But I was wrong." — Chow Mo-wan
"It's more real to me here than if I went up." — Newland Archer (paraphrase — closing voice-over of the film)
"Children don't live up to their parents' expectations. Let's just be happy that they're better than most." — Shukichi Hirayama
"I chose life." — Laura Brown

"It takes time in the morning for me to become George, time to adjust to what is expected of George and how he is to behave. By the time I have dressed and put the final layer of polish on the now slightly stiff but quite perfect George I know fully what part I'm supposed to play." — George Falconer (voice-over)
"I have every faith in it… as I have faith in relations between people." — Princess Ann (press-conference reply, when asked which European city she enjoyed most)
"I had a farm in Africa, at the foot of the Ngong Hills." — Karen Blixen (opening/closing narration)
"I cannot begin my day with a confrontation, please. I'm delivering the dress today, and I can't take up space with confrontation. I simply don't have time for confrontations." — Reynolds Woodcock
"I had no objections to your having a love affair. … I despised myself for loving you." — Walter Fane (paraphrase from Maugham source / film dialogue)

"Make no mistake — your relationships are the heaviest components in your life. Do you feel the straps cutting into your shoulders? … You don't need to carry all that weight. Why don't you set that bag down?" — Ryan Bingham

column4: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
column10: "OK." — Joel Barish (and Clementine, in answer to whether they can knowingly enter a relationship that may end the same way)

column7: Jung-won / 정원 (Han Suk-kyu / 한석규); Da-rim / 다림
column10: "내 기억 속에 무수한 사진들처럼 사랑도 언젠가는 추억으로 그친다는 걸 난 알고 있었습니다. 하지만 당신만은 추억이 되질 않았습니다. 사랑을 간직한 채 떠날 수 있게 해준 당신께 고맙단 말을 남깁니다." / "Like the countless photographs in my memory, I always knew love too eventually ends as memory. But you alone never became a memory. To you, who let me leave still holding love, I leave a word of thanks." — Jung-won, in his unsent letter

column7: Joo-hee / 주희 (Son Ye-jin / 손예진, in the 1968 timeline); Jun-ha / 준하 (Jo Seung-woo / 조승우); Tae-su / 태수
column10: "나 지금 어때 보여? … 나 지금 울고 있어, 눈물 안 보여? 왜 숨겼어, 앞을 못 본다는 거…" / "How do I look right now? … I'm crying — can't you see my tears? Why did you hide it from me — that you can't see…" — Joo-hee at the train station, then again at the reunion with the now-blind Jun-ha

column4: One Fine Spring Day / 봄날은 간다
column7: Sang-woo / 상우 (Yoo Ji-tae / 유지태); Eun-su / 은수
column10: "어떻게 사랑이 변하니?" / "How can love change?" — Sang-woo to Eun-su (and the closing scene's wordless turn-away)
"I'm the bad guy? How'd that happen? I did everything they told me to. Did you know I build missiles?" — Bill Foster
"Did you fuck my wife? … How could you ask me?" — Jake LaMotta / Joey LaMotta
"There are no two words in the English language more harmful than 'good job.'" — Terence Fletcher
"I have a competition in me. I want no one else to succeed. I hate most people." — Daniel Plainview
"No wire hangers, ever!" — Joan Crawford
"We're so hung up on this notion that we have some moral obligation here to take care of these people." — Derek Vinyard
"I have no feelings, and if I ever do, they won't defeat my intelligence." — Erika Kohut
"Even though I'm no more than a monster — don't I, too, have the right to live?" — Oh Dae-su
"By all means, move at a glacial pace. You know how that thrills me." — Miranda Priestly
"I will sell this house today. I will sell this house today." — Carolyn Burnham (then slapping herself)
"I'm as mad as hell, and I'm not going to take this anymore!" — Howard Beale
"I will practice believing my husband loves me, but I could be wrong." — Amy Dunne
"It's all gone to pot, hasn't it?" — Hugo Barrett

column4: Wages of Fear / Le Salaire de la peur
column10: "T'es plus rien, Jo. T'as la trouille, c'est tout." ("You're nothing, Jo. You're just scared, that's all.") — Mario
"그놈은 그냥 미끼를 던져분 것이고 자네 딸내미는 고것을 확 물어분 것이여." / "He just threw out the bait, and your daughter took it." — Il-gwang (일광)
"I'm Jack's complete lack of surprise." — The Narrator
"I just want to be perfect." — Nina Sayers
"Which would be worse — to live as a monster, or to die as a good man?" — Andrew Laeddis
"No, don't! Don't! No, I'm not like you. I don't feel as you do. I'm Sister Alma. I'm only here to help you. I'm not Elisabet Vogler. You are Elisabet Vogler." — Alma
"When there's no one left, the killer reveals himself." — Dr. Malick (paraphrase of therapy-room reveal)
"Hello, Doctor." — Eve Black (her first emergence in Dr. Luther's office)
"A boy's best friend is his mother." — Norman Bates
"It'll be just like in the movies. We'll pretend to be someone else." — "Betty" Elms
"I'm Vicky. I'm not Sybil. I never have been." — Vicky (alter)
"She's not my mum." — young Spider (paraphrase of repeated boyhood line)
"I must get this crack mended." — Carol Ledoux
"The hour between night and dawn … is when most people die, when sleep is deepest, when nightmares are most palpable." — Johan Borg

column4: A Tale of Two Sisters (Janghwa, Hongryeon)
column10: "Do you know what's really scary? You want to forget something. Totally wipe it off your mind. But you never can." — Eun-joo / Su-mi
"I'm the real one!" — "Mima" (the doppelgänger)
"If you were any thinner, you wouldn't exist." — Stevie

column7: Tae-suk / 태석 (Jae Hee / 재희) and Sun-hwa / 선화
column10: "우리가 사는 세상이 꿈인지 현실인지 알 수 없다." / "It is hard to tell if the world we live in is a dream or reality." — Closing intertitle
"엄마 있어요? 엄마 없어요?" / "Have you got a mother? Have you got a mom?" — Mother (in flashback to the abandoned witness, and ironically by the police about Do-joon's victim)
"여기는 어디고, 나는 누구인가." / "Where am I, and who am I?" (paraphrase / 의역 of the film's voice-over self-questioning during the crossing)
"You've never had a real loss, because that requires loving something more than you love yourself." — Sean Maguire (naming Will's intellectualizing defense)
"I have made the most important discovery of my life. It's only in the mysterious equations of love that any logical reasons can be found." — John Nash (Nobel speech)
"Sometimes it is the people no one imagines anything of who do the things that no one can imagine." — Alan Turing

column7: Dr. Strangelove (Peter Sellers); Gen. Buck Turgidson
column10: "Mr. President, I'm not saying we wouldn't get our hair mussed. But I do say no more than ten to twenty million killed, tops, depending on the breaks." — Gen. Buck Turgidson
"There should be no boundary to human endeavour. We are all different. However bad life may seem, there is always something you can do, and succeed at." — Stephen Hawking
"I think we should all get back into bed and be friends." — Alvy Singer (paraphrasing Marshall McLuhan); also: "Boy, if life were only like this!" — Alvy Singer
"It's one of my predecessors. Don't be afraid of it." — Bernard Berkman (about Kafka's *The Metamorphosis*)
"There are three ways to make a living in this business: be first, be smarter, or cheat." — John Tuld

column7: Brandon Shaw (John Dall); Phillip Morgan (Farley Granger); Rupert Cadell
column10: "Murder is — or should be — an art. And as such, the privilege of committing it should be reserved for those few who are really superior individuals." — Rupert Cadell (early in the film, glibly)
"If you figure out a way to live without serving a master, any master, then let the rest of us know, will you? For you'd be the first person in the history of the world." — Lancaster Dodd
"I think that New York is the new model for the new concentration camp, where the camp has been built by the inmates themselves, and the inmates are the guards." — Andre Gregory

column4: The Lives of Others / Das Leben der Anderen
column10: "Can anyone who has heard this music, I mean truly heard it, really be a bad person?" — Georg Dreyman (the line that pierces Wiesler's defense)

"In issue 141 of *The Fantastic Four*, published in November 1973, Reed Richards has to use his anti-matter weapon on his own son … [a] family is your own personal anti-matter." — Paul Hood (voiceover)
"I have a great murder story. A great plot. … I'm just curious what your reaction would be." — Judah Rosenthal
"No dream is ever just a dream." — Bill Harford
"마침내. 드디어, 울었다." / "At last. Finally, she cried." — Hae-jun's voice memo while watching Seo-rae through his telephoto lens
"I'm just an old man who likes dirty stories." / "더러운 이야기를 좋아하는 늙은이일 뿐이다." — Uncle Kouzuki

column4: Memoir of a Murderer / 살인자의 기억법
column10: "기억하지 말고 습관으로 만들어. 습관이 훨씬 더 오래간다." / "Don't memorize, make it habit. Habit lasts longer." — Kim Byung-soo (voice-over / diary)
"There is an idea of a Patrick Bateman, some kind of abstraction, but there is no real me, only an entity, something illusory … I simply am not there." — Patrick Bateman
"What's the most you ever lost on a coin toss?" — Anton Chigurh

"If I drive for you, you give me a time and a place. I give you a five-minute window. … Anything happens in that five minutes and I'm yours. No matter what. Anything a minute on either side of that and you're on your own." — The Driver
"I will give it consideration, but I do not feel I should accept the job for less than half a million dollars." — The Jackal
"There is no greater solitude than that of the samurai unless it is that of the tiger in the jungle … Perhaps." — Bushidō epigraph (Melville's own forgery, attributed to "Book of Bushido")

column7: Robert Graysmith (Jake Gyllenhaal); also Insp. Toschi
column10: "I need to know who he is. I need to stand there. I need to look him in the eye." — Robert Graysmith

column4: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
column10: "Our daughter started menstruating last week … She's the first in her class." — Anna Murphy (introducing the daughter at dinner)
"I want to build a normal life." — Marcello Clerici

column4: The Trial of Adolf Eichmann (Specialist) / Un Spécialiste
column10: "I never killed a Jew. I never killed a non-Jew. … I never gave such an order." — Adolf Eichmann (transcript)
"I think I want to be a lobster, sir. … Because lobsters live for over a hundred years … and I've always liked the sea." — David
"Greed, for lack of a better word, is good. Greed is right. Greed works." — Gordon Gekko
"What do you need? … Make it happen." — Karen Crowder (authorizing the murder)
"Mother died today. Or maybe yesterday, I don't know." — Meursault (opening voiceover, after Camus)
"Do not blink, Freddie. Do not blink." — Lancaster Dodd
"I have nothing to feel guilty about." — Georges Laurent
"시상이 떠오르려면, 사물을 잘 봐야 합니다. 잘 본다는 것이 중요해요." / "To find a poetic image, you must look carefully. To look carefully — that is the key." — poetry instructor Kim Yong-tak, internalized by Mi-ja
"밥은 먹고 다니냐?" / "You been eating your meals?" — Detective Park, to the prime suspect Park Hyun-gyu, after the lab results clear him

"내 기억 속의 무수한 사진들처럼 사랑도 추억으로 그렇게 남겨졌습니다. 당신을 사랑했습니다." / "Like the countless photographs in my memory, our love too was left behind as a memory. I loved you." — Jung-won (closing voice-over letter to Da-rim)
"How dare you talk to me that way! How dare you?" — Col. Frank Fitts (after Lester refuses the kiss)
"Listen, you fuckers, you screwheads. Here is a man who would not take it anymore. … Here is someone who stood up." — Travis Bickle
"Hate is baggage. Life's too short to be pissed off all the time. It's just not worth it." — Danny Vinyard (quoting reformed Derek)
"I look for John Proctor that took me from my sleep and put knowledge in my heart!" — Abigail Williams
"I can see your dirty pillows. Everyone will." — Margaret White
"I'm gonna beat this thing." — Frank Whitaker
"I'm only a servant, sir." — Hugo Barrett
"The weak are always trying to sabotage the strong." — Tracy Flick
"I have doubts. I have such doubts." — Sister Aloysius (final line)
"If we don't believe in our hearts that we're righteous people, we will not be able to walk together as Christians." — Rev. Shaw Moore
"I always thought it would be better, to be a fake somebody than a real nobody." — Tom Ripley
"There's something in the atmosphere that makes everything seem exaggerated." — Sister Clodagh

column4: The White Ribbon / Das weiße Band
column10: "We have decided to take measures … the white ribbon … should remind you of innocence and purity." — The Pastor
"There wasn't anything I could have done to save them." — Truman Capote
"You took them from your mother to give them to your boyfriend, didn't you?" — Sister Bridget
"내가 사람으로도 안 보이는 거 알아요. 그래도 이 아기는 당신 아기예요." / "I know you don't even think of me as human, but this baby is yours." — Eun-yi to Hoon
"그림은 거짓을 말하지 못한다." / "A painting cannot lie." — Kim Hong-do, to the disguised Yun-bok

"주 예수 그리스도의 이름으로 다음을 청하나이다… 살이 썩어 들어가는 문둥이처럼, 모두가 저를 피하게 하소서… 정조를 맹세한 이 몸이 더럽혀져 부끄러움 속에 살게 하소서." / "Grant me the following in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ. Like a leper rotting in flesh, let all avoid me… Ravage my body sworn to chastity; leave me with no pride, and have me live in shame." — Father Sang-hyun
"I can't beat it. I can't beat it. I'm sorry." — Lee Chandler
"No hay banda! There is no band! Il n'est pas de orquestra! It is all a tape recording. It is an illusion." — Magician at Club Silencio

column4: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
column10: "Please let me keep this memory, just this one." — Joel Barish
"I have to believe in a world outside my own mind. I have to believe that my actions still have meaning, even if I can't remember them." — Leonard Shelby
"Just get through the goddamn day." — George Falconer (paraphrase — voice-over recurring motif)

column4: Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith
column10: "I will not lose her the way I lost my mother." — Anakin Skywalker
"Tu me tues. Tu me fais du bien." ("You are killing me. You are doing me good.") — Elle
"The complexes that are buried in a sick mind … are like the curses that the gypsies put on you. You can free yourself only by remembering the thing you've buried." — Dr. Brulov
"Because! Because I was holding on, you son of a bitch! And he let go! Why did he let go?" — Conrad Jarrett
"It is not for me to display my feelings, even now." — Stevens (paraphrase of voice-over closing reflection)
"I am dead, although I live." — Isak Borg (paraphrase from dream-sequence narration)
"I was six years old. What does it have to do with me?" — Georges Laurent
"There. There now." — Marnie (regressed to childhood voice during the recovery scene; paraphrase of recovered-memory dialogue)
"Now I have only one thing left to do: nothing. I want no belongings, no memories. No friends, no love. Those are all traps." — Julie Vignon
"I knew. I knew of course. But the trick I always managed to perform … was not to allow myself to know." — Andrew Crocker-Harris
"다시 태어난다는 말, 전에는 그게 무슨 말인지 몰랐는데요, 이제는 확실히 알았어요." / "I never used to understand what 'born again' meant — now I know for certain." — Shin-ae at the prayer meeting, before the prison visit
"나 다시 돌아갈래!" / "I want to go back!" — Kim Yong-ho, on the railroad bridge
"If you were any thinner, you wouldn't exist." — Miller

column8: Saoirse Ronan / Romola Garai / Vanessa Redgrave
column10: "I gave them their happiness." — Briony Tallis
"Judy, please, it can't matter to you." — Scottie Ferguson
"We are all citizens of a different town now. A place with its own special rules and its own special laws." — Mitchell Stephens
"I could have got more out. I could have got more. I don't know. If I'd just… I could have got more." — Oskar Schindler
"If I grow up and turn out to be a… If I survive all this, you know what I'm gonna do? I'm gonna live in Bruges. Forever." — Ray
"After the awful deed is done, he finds that he's plagued by deep-rooted guilt… little sparks of his religious background, which he'd rejected, are suddenly stirred up." — Judah Rosenthal
"Wash it away! Wash it all away!" — Lady Asaji Washizu (paraphrase from English subtitled release)
"Out, damned spot! Out, I say!" — Lady Macbeth

column4: Crime and Punishment / Rikos ja rangaistus
column10: "I killed a louse." — Antti Rahikainen
"We bury our sins here, Dave. We wash them clean." — Jimmy Markum

column4: Through a Glass Darkly / Såsom i en spegel
column10: "I can't help feeling that this whole thing is somehow my fault." — David

column4: The Five Obstructions / De fem benspænd
column10: "We have to turn the perfect human into a real person." — Lars von Trier
"Indeed — why should I not be?" — Mr. Stevens
"I chose life." — Laura Brown

column4: Sympathy for Lady Vengeance / 친절한 금자씨
column10: "너나 잘하세요." / "You mind your own business." — Geum-ja, refusing absolution from a Christian missionary on her release

column4: Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter… and Spring / 봄 여름 가을 겨울 그리고 봄
column8: Kim Ki-duk himself, in the Winter chapter
column10: (largely silent film; the master's "Spring" injunction —) "네가 죽인 그 짐승들이, 평생 네 마음에 돌이 되어 따라다닐 것이다." / "Those creatures you killed will follow you as stones in your heart for the rest of your life." — The Old Master, to the boy
"내가 그 사람 아들 죽였잖아요. 그래서 내가 책임지러 온 거잖아요." / "I'm the one who killed her father. That's why I came — to take responsibility." — Hong Jong-du, to Gong-ju's brother
"Florals? For spring? Groundbreaking." — Miranda Priestly

"You are a worthless, friendless, faggot-lipped little piece of shit whose mommy left daddy when she figured out he wasn't Eugene O'Neill, and who is now weeping and slobbering all over my drum set like a fucking nine-year old girl!" — Terence Fletcher

"You have part of my attention — you have the minimum amount. The rest of my attention is back at the offices of Facebook, where my colleagues and I are doing things that no one in this room, including and especially your clients, are intellectually or creatively capable of doing." — Mark Zuckerberg
"I have a competition in me. I want no one else to succeed. I hate most people." — Daniel Plainview
"No... wire... hangers. What's wire hangers doing in this closet when I told you: no wire hangers EVER?" — Joan Crawford

"As I think you know, Alma, I prefer my asparagus with oil and salt. And knowing this, you've prepared the asparagus with butter. Now, I can imagine in certain circumstances being able to pretend that I like it made this way. Right now, I'm just admiring my own gallantry for eating it the way you've prepared it." — Reynolds Woodcock

"Let's all be 'truthsome'" — Violet Weston *(paraphrase from the film's truth-telling dinner sequence; verbatim "truth tellin'" tirade is well documented in the play but not on IMDb Quotes for the film)*

"If Bach's talent can be reduced to his gender, birth country, religion, sexuality, and so on, then so can yours." — Lydia Tár *(paraphrase, public Juilliard scene; verbatim transcript widely reproduced in coverage)*
"I *am* big. It's the *pictures* that got small." — Norma Desmond

column4: The Bridge on the River Kwai
column10: "I hate the British! You are defeated but you have no shame. You are stubborn but you have no pride. You endure but you have no courage." — Colonel Saito
"That's all you were good for." — Helge *(paraphrase of the verbatim Danish line "Det var det eneste, I duede til," widely cited in Festen criticism)*
"Pig fuck!" — Lancaster Dodd
"You're not a victim of me. You're a victim of your *ambition*." — Sandra Voyter *(paraphrase; verbatim phrasing varies between French and English court-recording subtitles)*

column4: Burnt by the Sun / Утомлённые солнцем
column10: "You are nothing now." — Mitya *(paraphrase; the Russian dialogue is closely tracked in Mitchell, "Fire and Water Imagery in Mikhalkov's *Burnt by the Sun*")*
"I'm a victim too! I'm a victim of my own bloody instincts!" — Tomas
"근데 그 냄새가 선을 넘지." / "But that smell crosses the line. It powers through right into the back seat." — 박동익 (Park Dong-ik)
"남자들은 정말 바보야. 식모한테 마음을 주다니!" / "Men are such fools. Taking interest in a maid!" — 김부인 (Mrs. Kim)
"Judy, please, it can't matter to you." — Scottie Ferguson (insisting on changing the color of her hair)
"I love her smile. I love her hair. I love her knees. I love how she licks her lips before she talks. I love her heart-shaped birthmark on her neck. I love it when she sleeps." — Tom Hansen
"Can't repeat the past?" — Jay Gatsby
"I'm always gonna love you." — Sebastian Wilder (and Mia in reply)
"I'm stuck. Does it get easier? […] No. Yes. It gets easier." — Bob Harris *(verbatim from the film, hotel-bar exchange about marriage; widely transcribed)*
"You alone can make my song take flight." — The Phantom *(from the film's "Music of the Night")*
"I have a love in my life. It makes me stronger than anything you can imagine." — Barry Egan
"I've never loved anyone the way I love you." — Theodore (and Samantha responds, "Me too. Now we know how.")

"Get out of here! Go back to Rome. Whatever you do, love it, the way you loved the projection booth when you were a little squirt." — Alfredo (the line that immortalizes Elena as Totò's untouchable past)

column4: In the Mood for Love / 花樣年華
column7: Chow Mo-wan (Tony Leung) and Su Li-zhen
column10: "It is a restless moment. She has kept her head lowered, to give him a chance to come closer. But he could not, for lack of courage. She turns and walks away." — closing intertitle

column4: Amélie / Le Fabuleux destin d'Amélie Poulain
column10: "If you let this chance pass, eventually, your heart will become as dry and brittle as my skeleton." — Dufayel (the line that names Amélie's idealized avoidance)

column4: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
column10: "Please let me keep this memory, just this one." — Joel Barish
"I thought, my God, you know how you're always tryin' to get things to come out perfect in art because, uh, it's real difficult in life." — Alvy Singer
"Call me by your name and I'll call you by mine." — Oliver (the line that fuses identity with idealization)

column7: Tenoch Iturbide (Diego Luna) and Julio Zapata
column10: "La vida es como la espuma, así que dale a la mar." — Luisa *(verbatim, "Life is like the surf, so give yourselves to the sea")*

"내가 품위 있댔죠? 품위가 어디에서 나오는지 알아요? 자부심이에요. 난 자부심 있는 경찰이었어요. 그런데 여자에 미쳐서… 수사를 망쳤죠. 나는요… 완전히 붕괴됐어요." / "You said I had dignity? Do you know where dignity comes from? Pride. I was a proud cop. But I went mad over a woman… and I ruined the investigation. I am… completely shattered." — 해준 (Hae-jun)

"내 기억 속에서 무수히 잊혀져 간 사람들처럼, 당신도 추억이 될 것이 두려웠습니다. 그러나 사랑을 간직한 채 떠날 수 있게 해 준 당신께 감사드립니다." / "Like the countless people who have been forgotten in my memory, I was afraid you too would become only a memory. But thank you for letting me leave still holding the love." — 정원's voice-over letter to 다림

column4: Bungee Jumping of Their Own / 번지점프를 하다
column10: "사람이 죽으면 어디로 가는지 알아? 사랑하는 사람의 가슴 속으로 간대." / "Do you know where people go when they die? They say they go into the heart of the person who loved them." — 인우 (In-woo)
"Dear Wheeler: you provide the prose poems. I'll provide the war." — Charles Foster Kane

"This right here is the land of opportunity. This is America. This is my home! The show goes on! […] They're gonna need to send in the National Guard to take me out, cos I ain't going nowhere!" — Jordan Belfort
"The world is yours, chico." — Tony Montana (recurring motif and dirigible message)

"If you have a milkshake, and I have a milkshake, and I have a straw. There it is, that's a straw, you see? Watch it. Now, my straw reaches acroooooooss the room and starts to drink your milkshake. I... drink... your... milkshake! I drink it up!" — Daniel Plainview

column4: Aguirre, the Wrath of God / Aguirre, der Zorn Gottes
column10: "If I, Aguirre, want the birds to drop dead from the trees, then the birds will drop dead from the trees. I am the Wrath of God." — Don Lope de Aguirre

"You have no right to call me a murderer, you have a right to kill me. You have a right to do that. But you have no right to judge me. […] If I had 10 divisions of those men, then our troubles would be over here very quickly." — Colonel Kurtz
"I'm as mad as hell, and I'm not going to take this anymore!" — Howard Beale
"I am the father of Africa." — Idi Amin
"Steiner's assault was an order!" — Adolf Hitler (the bunker map-room scene)

"I came here to say that I do not recognize anyone's right to one minute of my life. Nor to any part of my energy. Nor to any achievement of mine. No matter who makes the claim, how large their number or how great their need." — Howard Roark
"Vanity, definitely my favorite sin." — John Milton

column7: 정청 Jung Chung (Hwang Jung-min / 황정민) and 이중구 Lee Joong-gu
column10: "거 죽기 딱 좋은 날씨네." / "It's a perfect day to die." — 정청 (Jung Chung) ; and "살려는 드릴게." / "I'll let you live." — 이자성 to 이중구 in the elevator

"어차피 대중들은 개, 돼지입니다. 거 뭐 하러 개, 돼지들한테 신경을 쓰시고 그러십니까? 적당히 짖어대다가 알아서 조용해질 겁니다." / "The masses are dogs and pigs anyway. Why bother caring what dogs and pigs think? They'll bark for a while and quiet down on their own." — 이강희 (Lee Kang-hee)

column7: 황 회장 Chairman Hwang (Cheon Ho-jin / 천호진) and 병두 Byung-doo
column10: "병두야, 세상에서 성공하려면 딱 두 가지만 알면 돼. 자기한테 필요한 사람이 누군지, 그리고 그 사람이 뭘 필요로 하는지." / "Byung-doo, to succeed in this world you only need to know two things — who is useful to you, and what that person needs." — 황 회장 (Chairman Hwang)
"You don't understand, there's nothin'... there's nothin' there. There's nothin' there." — Lee Chandler

"I don't want to see any doctors or counselors. I'm me! This is *my* family. And if we have problems we will solve those problems in the privacy of our own home, not by running to some kind of specialist every time something goes wrong." — Beth Jarrett
"I don't need any help from anyone. And I'm not going to leave my flat. All I want is for everyone to fuck off." — Anthony
"Sera... what you don't understand is — no, see, no. You can never, never ask me to stop drinking. Do you understand?" — Ben Sanderson
"I'm not a minor, Mr. Brophy, and just to ease your conscience, I'm buying these to refill my cigarette lighter." — Don Birnam
"I think it's ridiculous, I think it's bullshit, and I..." — Dr. John Howland
(paraphrase) Sara repeatedly insists "I'm gonna be on television" and refuses her son Harry's pleas to stop the pills. (No verbatim line on this point is published on the IMDb /quotes/ page.)
"Tomorrow evening Melancholia will pass us by and you'll never have to see it again, okay?" — John
"But things are getting better every day." — Georges
"Look, I did know she was pregnant, but in that moment I didn't, I had forgotten. This matter was not on my mind at all." — Nader

(paraphrase) Georges insists "I have nothing to reproach myself for" and tells his wife he found no one at the apartment — both lies the film exposes. (Verbatim quotes from this scene are not present on the IMDb /quotes/ page.)

(paraphrase) Theo tells Lucas the village must believe Klara because "children don't lie" — a creed that survives all later disconfirmation. (No verbatim line on this point is published on IMDb /quotes/.)
"Are there things I should know and don't?" — Raimunda / "Oodles." — Sole
"How are you helping me, huh? […] You're a burden. You're just dragging me down. […] Stop playing the victim." — Brandon Sullivan

(paraphrase) Ofelia narrates the fairy tale to her unborn brother — "A long time ago, in the underground realm…" — and addresses the Faun rather than her dying mother. (No verbatim quote published on IMDb /quotes/ tied to denial of trauma.)

"내가 아직 그를 용서한다는 말을 하지도 않았는데, 그는 하나님으로부터 이미 용서받았다고 합디다. 어떻게 그럴 수가 있어요? 내가 용서를 안 했는데, 어떻게 하나님이 먼저 그를 용서할 수가 있느냐고요!" / "I hadn't even said I forgave him, and he told me God had already forgiven him. How can that be? I haven't forgiven him — how can God forgive him before I do?" — 신애 (Shin-ae)

"우리 아들은 그런 짓 할 애가 아니에요." / "My son is not the kind of boy who would do that." — 어머니 (Mother) ; and the closing line, "너, 나쁜 기억 있으면 풀어버리는 침자리 있다 했지." / "I told you there's an acupuncture point that can release bad memories, didn't I."
"나 다시 돌아갈래!" / "I want to go back!" — 영호 (Yong-ho), screaming at the train
"I can see your dirty pillows. Everyone will." — Margaret White
"No, this is different. She's after me. She's trying to replace me!" — Nina

"All the animals come out at night: whores, skunk pussies, buggers, queens, fairies, dopers, junkies... sick, venal. Someday a real rain will come and wash all this scum off the streets." — Travis Bickle
"Decent hard-working Americans like my dad are getting rubbed out by social parasites. […] Blacks, Browns, Yellow whatever." — Derek Vinyard
"When he was sixteen, we had a fight. Hit me in the jaw — a big kid. Haven't seen him for two years. Kids... work your heart out…" — Juror #3
"What scared me wasn't that he'd pushed me. What scared me was how much he wanted to hurt me more. […] I am frightened of my own husband." — Amy Dunne (V.O., diary fabrication)
"I don't feel I have to wipe everybody out, Tom. Just my enemies." — Michael Corleone
"I wonder whether your madness isn't the worst kind. You act healthy, act it so well that everyone believes you — everyone except me, because I know how rotten you are." — Sister Alma
"I don't have feelings. And if I ever did, they would not defeat my intelligence!" — Erika Kohut

(paraphrase) Georges insists to his wife and his employer that "this man" — Majid — is trying to ruin him, and that he himself has nothing to feel guilty about. (No verbatim line on this exchange is published on the IMDb /quotes/ page.)

column7: Detective Takabe (Kōji Yakusho) and the hypnotist Mamiya
column10: (paraphrase) Mamiya tells subjects, "I used to exist inside myself, but now I'm only on the outside — that's why I can see what's inside of you," and asks each interlocutor "who are you?" until they enact a buried wish to kill. (No verbatim line published on IMDb /quotes/.)

(paraphrase, from Shakespeare) "O, beware, my lord, of jealousy; / It is the green-eyed monster which doth mock / The meat it feeds on." — Iago to Othello (verbatim from Shakespeare; Parker's film uses these lines, but the IMDb /quotes/ page does not list them)

(paraphrase) Fletcher insists "there are no two words in the English language more harmful than 'good job,'" framing his sadism as the necessary cure for student mediocrity. (Already cited under devaluation; this mechanism reading focuses on projection rather than devaluation.)

(paraphrase) Frank shouts "you're like me!" and "Don't you fucking look at me!" at Jeffrey during the kidnapping/beating sequence. (No exact line on this exchange is published on the IMDb /quotes/ page.)

(paraphrase) Klara's stepfather Theo and the headmistress repeatedly insist "children don't lie about these things" — making Klara's confused fragment carry the weight of every adult's projected dread. (No verbatim line on IMDb /quotes/.)
"그놈은 그냥 미끼를 던진 거고, 자네 딸은 그 미끼를 문 거야. 자네 딸이 뭘 잘못해서 이런 일을 당하는 게 아녀." / "He's just fishing. Not even he knows what he'll catch. He just threw out the bait, and your daughter took it." — 일광 (Il-Gwang)

column7: 박두만 Park Doo-man (Song Kang-ho / 송강호) and 조용구 Cho Yong-koo
column10: "밥은 먹고 다니냐?" / "Do you eat your meals properly?" — 박두만 (Park Doo-man) to suspect Park Hyun-gyu ; and "내 눈을 봐. 나는 눈빛만 봐도 다 알아." / "Look at my eyes. I can tell everything just from a person's eyes." — 박두만
"내가 죽였어요." / "I killed them." — 영민 (Young-min) ; and Joong-ho's earlier conviction, "이 새끼가 우리 애들 팔아먹었어." / "This bastard sold off my girls." — 중호

"Son, we live in a world that has walls, and those walls have to be guarded by men with guns... my existence, while grotesque and incomprehensible to you, *saves lives*... I would rather you just said 'thank you' and went on your way." — Col. Jessep
"There's no nobility in poverty. I have been a rich man and I have been a poor man. And I choose rich every fuckin' time." — Jordan Belfort

"And then one morning, he awakens. The sun is shining, his family is around him and mysteriously, the crisis has lifted... In fact, he prospers. The killing gets attributed to another person... so I mean, what the hell? One more doesn't even matter. Now he's scott-free." — Judah Rosenthal
"The innocent are sometimes slain to make way for a grander scheme. You were collateral damage." — Chris Wilton
"That's the beauty of argument, if you argue correctly, you're never wrong." — Nick Naylor
"We were guards. Our job was to guard the prisoners. We couldn't just let them escape... Well, what would you have done?" — Hanna Schmitz
"It's just money; it's made up... It's all just the same thing over and over; we can't help ourselves. And you and I can't control it, or stop it, or even slow it." — John Tuld
"Tell me the difference between stupid and illegal and I'll have my wife's brother arrested." — Jared Vennett
"This would sharpen you up and make you ready for a bit of the old ultra-violence." — Alex (voiceover)
"The officer told me to. He said it was police procedure." — Sandra (paraphrase; based on character's repeated on-camera justifications throughout the film)
"We're a billion-dollar company... we just have to manage the situation." — Karen Crowder (paraphrase; rehearsed-statement scenes)
"It was an accident. A drunken driver." — The General (paraphrase; central plot mechanism of the film)
"None of this would've happened if Mr. McAllister hadn't messed with my campaign... I deserve this." — Tracy Flick (paraphrase; voiceover composite)
"Last night a fairy visited me." — Ofelia, to Mercedes
"Just close your eyes, but keep your mind wide open." — Leslie Burke
"She's a missionary." — Lars Lindstrom (introducing Bianca to his brother Gus) Source (paraphrase confirmed via shooting script discussion): https://www.instagram.com/reel/DLkqq8ZzTWm/ (paraphrase)
"Stéphane TV. Disasters, catastrophes, and meteorological forecasts." — Stéphane (paraphrase)
"Soy Ana." ("I am Ana.") — Ana, calling out to the spirit

column7: Pauline Parker (Melanie Lynskey) and Juliet Hulme
column10: "I'm going to the Fourth World. It's sort of like Heaven, only better, because there aren't any Christians." — Juliet Hulme
"And Max, the king of all wild things, was lonely and wanted to be where someone loved him best of all." — Narration
"You mean she'd rather imagine herself relating to an absent person than build relationships with those around her?" — Raphaël Poulain (paraphrase / English subtitles)

column4: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
column10: "Walter is constitutionally incapable of standing up for himself, so he retreats into his fantasy world, where he is heroic, poised, self-assured, and the master of his fate." — IMDb plot synopsis (describing Mitty)
"If I shut my eyes and try hard enough, maybe I'll wake inside your dream." — Jeliza-Rose
"Are you trying to save me?" — Alexandria, to Roy (paraphrase)
"Why are you wearing that stupid bunny suit?" — Donnie Darko
"There are nearly thirteen million people in the world. None of those people is an extra. They're all the leads of their own stories." — Ellen Bascomb (Caden's set actress)
"I can't imagine you with all your complexity, all your perfection, all your imperfection. Look at you. You are just a shade of my real wife." — Dom Cobb, to projected Mal

column4: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
column10: "Meet me… in Montauk." — Clementine (in Joel's memory)
"우리가 사는 세상이 꿈인지 현실인지 알 수 없다." / "We can't tell whether the world we live in is a dream or reality." — closing intertitle

column4: Save the Green Planet! / 지구를 지켜라!
column10: "안드로메다 PK45 행성의 외계인이 지구를 침공하려 한다." / "Aliens from planet PK45 in Andromeda are about to invade Earth." — 병구

column4: Castaway on the Moon / 김씨 표류기
column7: Mr. Kim / 김씨 (Jung Jae-young / 정재영) and Hyo-min Kim / 김씨
column10: "후 아 유?" / "Who are you?" — 여자 김씨, written in the sand of her bottle-letters; and "죽는 건 언제라도 할 수 있습니다." / "Dying is something I can always do later." — 남자 김씨

column7: Nina Sayers (Natalie Portman) ← Erica (Barbara Hershey) and Lily
column10: "It's my turn to be the Black Swan!" — Nina Sayers

column7: Nurse Alma (Bibi Andersson) and Elisabet Vogler
column10: "No! I'm not like you. I don't feel like you." — Alma, to/about Elisabet
"When I was at school, if one of us had had some bad news or was a bit down, we used to stroke each other." — Barbara Covett
"There are no two words in the English language more harmful than 'good job.'" — Terence Fletcher
"I want you flat on your back. Helpless, tender, open with only me to help. And then I want you strong again. You're not going to die." — Alma
"You never loved me. You just loved how much I loved you." — Mother

column7: Erika Kohut (Isabelle Huppert) ← her mother (Annie Girardot) and Walter Klemmer
column10: "I went for a walk. Do you mind? I spent 8 hours in my cage. I was tired and needed some air." — Erika Kohut, to her mother
"He's gonna laugh at you. They're all gonna laugh at you!" — Margaret White
"There's nothing in your head that you haven't said!" — Fanni

column7: Lancaster Dodd (Philip Seymour Hoffman) ← Freddie Quell
column10: "If you figure a way to live without serving a master, any master, then let the rest of us know, will you? For you'd be the first person in the history of the world." — Lancaster Dodd

column7: Hedra "Hedy" Carlson (Jennifer Jason Leigh) ← Allison Jones
column10: "She borrowed my hairstyle." — Allison Jones (paraphrase)
"I think I just spent the evening with my ex-husband." — Albert (paraphrase)
"I will practice believing my husband loves me, but I could be wrong." — Amy Dunne (Cool Girl monologue / diary)
"I'm nobody's servant." — Hugo Barrett (paraphrase / Pinter-Losey screenplay)

column4: The Hand That Rocks the Cradle
column7: Peyton Flanders (Rebecca De Mornay) ← Claire Bartel
column10: "When you hold them to your breast, they only love you. When they cry, you can soothe them." — Peyton (paraphrase)
"너, 엄마 없어..?" / "Don't you have a mother…?" — 혜자, to the junkman before bludgeoning him
"우리가 싸우는 건 세상을 바꾸기 위해서가 아니라 세상이 우리를 바꾸지 못하게 하기 위해서예요." / "We fight not to change the world, but to keep the world from changing us." — 인호
"I am Jack's complete lack of surprise." — The Narrator
"I just want to be perfect." — Nina Sayers
"A boy's best friend is his mother." — Norman Bates
"No hay banda. There is no band. Il n'y a pas d'orchestre. … It is all a tape." — Magician at Club Silencio
"I used to think that my life was a tragedy, but now I realize it's a comedy." — Arthur Fleck
"I will practice believing my husband loves me, but I could be wrong." — Amy Dunne
"I won't be ignored, Dan." — Alex Forrest
"How do you do, Doctor? I'm Eve Black." — Eve Black, on first emergence
"No! I'm not like you. I don't feel like you." — Alma (paraphrase from screenplay)
"Did you fuck my brother?" — Jake LaMotta
"Don't go… I must repair the leak." — Carol Ledoux (paraphrase / sparse dialogue) (paraphrase)

column4: Sympathy for Lady Vengeance / 친절한 금자씨
column10: "너나 잘하세요." / "Mind your own business." — 금자, to the prison chaplain offering tofu

column7: Father Sang-hyun / 상현 (Song Kang-ho / 송강호) and Tae-ju / 태주
column10: "내가 이 지옥에서 데리고 나가 줄게요." / "I'll take you out of this hell." — 상현, to 태주, early; later: "저는 이제 모든 쾌락을 갈구합니다." / "Now I crave every pleasure."
"You talkin' to me? You talkin' to me? You talkin' to me? Then who the hell else are you talkin' to? You talkin' to me? Well I'm the only one here." — Travis Bickle

"There was me, that is Alex, and my three droogs… and we sat in the Korova Milkbar trying to make up our rasoodocks what to do with the evening." — Alex (opening voice-over) (paraphrase from Burgess novel/film) (paraphrase)

column4: We Need to Talk About Kevin
column10: "I used to think I knew. Now I'm not so sure." — Kevin Khatchadourian, on motive
"Sometimes I'd tell them the truth and they still wouldn't believe me, so I prefer to lie." — Antoine Doinel, to the psychiatrist
"Choose life. Choose a job. Choose a career. Choose a family… But why would I want to do a thing like that?" — Mark Renton (opening voice-over)
"I killed those guys because they were awful. Everybody is awful these days. It's enough to make anyone crazy." — Arthur Fleck, on Murray Franklin
"You want a fair chance, don't you? You bet with us. You're on their side. You want them to win." — Paul, to camera
"I'm a natural born killer." — Mickey Knox
"I fuck you right where you breathe, because I don't give two shits about you or nobody else." — Johnny Boy
"You're tearing me apart!" — Jim Stark, to his parents
"Virgins. I love 'em. No diseases, no loose-as-a-goose pussy, no skank. No nothin'." — Telly
"I'm so sorry, I did so many bad things." — The Lieutenant
"넌.. 너무 불친절혀." / "You… are just too unkind." — 복남, after the killings, to Hae-won
"씨발, 내가 미친 새끼지. 내가 미친 새끼야." / "Fuck — I'm a crazy bastard, I'm a crazy bastard." — 상훈, after striking his sister
"You don't understand, there's nothin'... there's nothin' there. There's nothin' there." — Lee Chandler
"I don't want to leave." — Bob Harris
"The earth is evil. We don't need to grieve for it." — Justine
"Loneliness has followed me my whole life. Everywhere. In bars, in cars, sidewalks, stores, everywhere. There's no escape. I'm God's lonely man." — Travis Bickle (voiceover diary)
"Dying is too scary, but I wish I could disappear like I had never existed." — Hae-mi
"I won't settle for anything less than the brutal truth. Brutal. Brutal. Each day I'll hand you a paper, it'll tell you what happened to you that day." — Caden Cotard
"(paraphrase) I must get this crack mended." — Carol Ledoux (in one of her last lucid utterances; verifiable verbatim subtitle text not located on IMDb Quotes)
"Believe me, Anna, words are more and more pointless. They create misunderstandings." — Sandro to Anna, just before she vanishes
"The more we talk, the less the words mean." — Nana
"You ain't even human no more. Workin' apart from folks so long. You're only tolerable when you're drunk." — Thomas Howard to Wake

column4: A Few Days from the Life of I.I. Oblomov
column10: "(paraphrase) Why should I get up? What is there worth getting up for?" — Oblomov (paraphrase from Goncharov source novel; specific verbatim film line not located on IMDb Quotes)
"Well, I would say that I'm just drifting. Here in the pool." — Benjamin Braddock
"What they call passion actually is not some emotional energy, but just the friction between their souls and the outside world." — Stalker (about the Writer's class)
"All of a sudden, I miss everyone..." — Mabel Longhetti
"사과 한 알을 진짜로 본다는 건 어떤 걸까요?" / "What would it mean to really see one apple?" — Mi-ja's writing teacher, a line Mi-ja silently inhabits

"내 기억속에 무수한 사진들처럼 사랑도 언젠가는 추억으로 그친다는 걸 난 알고 있었습니다. 하지만 당신만은 추억이 되질 않았습니다." / "I knew that, like the countless photos in my memory, love would one day end as memory. But you alone never became memory." — 정원, voiceover letter

column8: Kim Hee-yeon / 김희연 and Kim Song-hee / 김송희
column10: "언니, 나무 찾았어." / "Unni, I found a tree." — 빈; "그거 죽은 거잖아." / "That's a dead one." — 진; "아니야." / "No it isn't." — 빈
"He pushes people away before they get a chance to leave him. It's a defense mechanism. And for 20 years he's been alone because of that." — Sean Maguire (about Will)
"Dr. Green, how can you diagnose someone as an obsessive compulsive disorder, and then act like I have some choice about barging in here?" — Melvin Udall
"I can't beat it. I can't beat it. I'm sorry." — Lee Chandler
"I wanted to ask you something because you're a doctor... I don't like myself sometimes. As I just said that out loud I realize it sounded a little strange but it's not." — Barry Egan
"I don't think so. No. Definitely not." — Raymond Babbitt (Charlie's negative responses mirror Raymond's stereotyped refusal)
"Joan, let me ask you something. All that work I did at the end of our marriage, making dinners, cleaning up, being more attentive. It never was going to make a difference, was it?" — Bernard Berkman
"Oh yes, very undateable." — Frances
"Baby steps onto the elevator… baby steps into the elevator… I'm in the elevator." — Bob Wiley (cycling through "baby steps" while creating new helplessness)

"I feel that life is divided into the horrible and the miserable. That's the two categories. […] So you should be thankful that you're miserable, because that's very lucky, to be miserable." — Alvy Singer
"(paraphrase) I don't know what you want from me, Calvin. I don't know what *anyone* wants from me." — Beth Jarrett (paraphrase; verifiable verbatim line not located on IMDb Quotes)
"(paraphrase) She didn't want me. My grandmother took me in." — Antoine Doinel (paraphrase from interview scene; subtitled French; verifiable English IMDb Quotes line not located)

"(paraphrase) Although I had been told the truth about myself, I felt strangely relieved." — Isak Borg (paraphrase from Bergman's published screenplay; specific verbatim film line not available on IMDb Quotes)
"I don't need her or anyone else. I can manage very well on my own." — Anthony
"I'd like a plain omelette, no potatoes, tomatoes instead, a cup of coffee, and wheat toast." — Bobby Dupea (the diner scene escalating into rejection of every accommodation)
"We've gone on holiday by mistake!" — Withnail
"나 다시 돌아갈래!" / "I want to go back!" — 영호, on the bridge before the oncoming train
"The thing is, Bob, it's not that I'm lazy, it's that I just don't care… It's a problem of motivation, all right?" — Peter Gibbons
"I regard this room as my private place of work and I — I prefer to keep distractions to a minimum." — Stevens
"Don't worry, I can't be bothered! You're not worth the trouble it would take to hit you! You're not worth the powder it'd take to blow you up!" — Frank Wheeler
"Truth is you just can't compete with a younger woman. It's just one of those unfair things in life." — Violet Weston (to Barbara, about Bill)
"My husband has spent the entire afternoon DRYING THINGS!" — Penelope Longstreet
"You're being so much like your father… I didn't compare you to him. I said you were acting like him." — Nicole Barber
"I don't like myself sometimes. Can you help me?" — Barry Egan
"(paraphrase) I have a different memory of what happened than you do." — Tomas (subtitle/screenplay rendering)

column7: The Klingenfeldt family, esp. Helge (Henning Moritzen) and Michael
column10: "(paraphrase) Nice speech, Christian. Now perhaps you'll go so we can eat our breakfast." — Michael
"I hope that was an empty bottle, George! You can't afford to waste good liquor, not on YOUR salary!" — Martha

column7: Joan Berkman (Laura Linney) and Frank Berkman
column10: "Suck my dick, ass man." — Frank Berkman (to his father)

column7: Winfried Conradi / "Toni Erdmann" (Peter Simonischek) and Ines Conradi
column10: "(paraphrase) Are you actually human?" — Winfried, to Ines
"(paraphrase) I'm not going. I won't say it again." — Nader
"I'm not a heartless person, that's why I cried when my dog died." — David, undermining his own facade

column8: Jane Adams, Lara Flynn Boyle, Cynthia Stevenson
column10: "I'm not laughing at you, I'm laughing with you." — Andy (to Joy, in the breakup that opens the film)

"왜 내 애만 죽여요? 이 집 남자는 애를 배게 하고 이 집 여자는 애를 떼게 하고. 내 몸은 장난감처럼 뭘 해도 좋아요?" / "Why must only my child die? The man of this house gets me pregnant; the woman of this house makes me abort. Is my body a toy you can do anything with?" — 하녀
"은희야, 힘들고 우울할 땐 손가락을 봐. 그리고 한 손가락 한 손가락 움직여." / "Eun-hee, when you're tired and sad, look at your fingers. Then move them, one by one." — 영지
"저는 그냥 혼자가 편해요." / "I'm just more comfortable alone." — 진아
